questions.question: What is the concentration, in mg per mL of a solution containing 4 mEq of potassium chloride (KCl) per mL? (KCl molecular weight = 74.5)

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of KCl is 1 because it has 1 positive charge and 1 negative charge. From the question, we know that the MW of KCl is 74.5 mg, so 1 mEq = 74.5 mg / 1 = 74.5 mg

steps.text: Use ratio and proportion to convert between mEq and mg
steps.hint: 1 mEq = 74.5 mg, so 4 mEq = 4 * 74.5 mg = 298 mg

questions.question: What is the concentration, in mg per mL of a solution containing 2 mEq of ammonium chloride (NH<sub>4</sub>Cl) per mL? (NH<sub>4</sub>Cl  molecular weight is 53.5).

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of NH<sub>4</sub>Cl is 1 because it has 1 positive charge and 1 negative charge. From the question, we know that the MW of NH<sub>4</sub>Cl is 53.5 mg, so 1 mEq = 53.5 mg / 1 = 53.5 mg

steps.text: Use ratio and proportion to convert between mEq and mg
steps.hint: 1 mEq = 53.5 mg, so 2 mEq = 2 * 53.5 mg = 107 mg

questions.question: What is the concentration, in mg per mL of a solution containing 3 mEq of calcium chloride (CaCl<sub>2</sub> ∙ 2 H<sub>2</sub>O) per mL? (CaCl<sub>2</sub> ∙ 2 H<sub>2</sub>O molecular weight = 147)

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of CaCl<sub>2</sub> ∙ 2 H<sub>2</sub>O is 2 because calcium has a +2 charge. From the question, we know that the MW of CaCl<sub>2</sub> ∙ 2 H<sub>2</sub>O is 147 mg, so 1 mEq = 147 mg / 2 = 73.5 mg

steps.text: Use ratio and proportion to convert between mEq and mg
steps.hint: 1 mEq = 73.5 mg, so 3 mEq = 3 * 73.5 mg = 220.5 mg

questions.question: What is the concentration, in grams per milliliter, of a solution containing 7 mEq of calcium chloride (CaCl<sub>2</sub> ∙2H<sub>2</sub>O) per milliliter? (CaCl<sub>2</sub> ∙2H<sub>2</sub>O molecular weight is 147). Round the final answer to the nearest hundredth.

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of CaCl<sub>2</sub> ∙2H<sub>2</sub>O is 2 because calcium has a +2 charge. From the question, we know that the MW of CaCl<sub>2</sub> ∙2H<sub>2</sub>O is 147 mg, so 1 mEq = 147 mg / 2 = 73.5 mg

steps.text: Use ratio and proportion to convert between mEq and mg
steps.hint: 1 mEq = 73.5 mg, so 7 mEq = 7 * 73.5 mg = 514.5 mg

steps.text: Convert mg/mL to g/mL
steps.hint: Convert mg to g: 514.5 mg ÷ 1000 mg/g = 0.5145 g/mL, which rounds to 0.51 g/mL to the nearest hundredth

questions.question: A solution contains 447 mg of KCl per mL. Express this concentration in terms of mEq/mL of KCl (KCl molecular weight is 74.5).

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of KCl is 1 because it has 1 positive charge and 1 negative charge. From the question, we know that the MW of KCl is 74.5 mg, so 1 mEq = 74.5 mg / 1 = 74.5 mg

steps.text: Use ratio and proportion to convert between mg and mEq
steps.hint: 1 mEq/74.5 mg = X mEq/447 mg. X = 447 mg ÷ 74.5 mg/mEq = 6 mEq/mL

questions.question: A solution contains 304 mg of FeSO<sub>4</sub> per ml. Express this concentration in terms of mEq/mL of FeSO<sub>4</sub> (FeSO<sub>4</sub> molecular weight is 152).

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of FeSO<sub>4</sub> is 2 because iron has a +2 charge. From the question, we know that the MW of FeSO<sub>4</sub> is 152 mg, so 1 mEq = 152 mg / 2 = 76 mg

steps.text: Use ratio and proportion to convert between mg and mEq
steps.hint: 1 mEq/76 mg = X mEq/304 mg. X = 304 mg ÷ 76 mg/mEq = 4 mEq/mL

questions.question: A 20-mL ampule of potassium chloride contains 5.96 g of KCl. What is the concentration of the solution in terms of milliequivalents per milliliter? (KCl molecular weight is 74.5)

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of KCl is 1 because it has 1 positive charge and 1 negative charge. From the question, we know that the MW of KCl is 74.5 mg, so 1 mEq = 74.5 mg / 1 = 74.5 mg.

steps.text: Use ratio and proportion to convert between g/mL and mEq/mL
steps.hint: First convert 5.96 g/20 mL to g/mL: 5.96 g ÷ 20 mL = 0.298 g/mL. Then convert to mg/mL: 0.298 g/mL × 1000 mg/g = 298 mg/mL. Finally convert to mEq/mL: 298 mg/mL ÷ 74.5 mg/mEq = 4 mEq/mL.

questions.question: A person is to receive 26 mg of ammonium chloride per kg of body weight. If the person weighs 165 pounds, how many milliliters of a sterile solution of ammonium chloride containing 0.7 mEq/mL should be administered? (NH<sub>4</sub>Cl molecular weight is 53.5).

steps.text: Convert pounds to kilograms and calculate total mg needed
steps.hint: Convert the patient's weight from pounds to kilograms, then multiply by the dose per kg to get the total mg needed. 165 lb / 2.2 = 75 kg. 26 mg/kg × 75 kg = 1950 mg.

steps.text: Convert 0.7 mEq/mL to mg/mL
steps.hint: 1 mEq = 53.5 mg (valence of NH4Cl = 1). 1 mEq / 53.5 mg = 0.7 mEq / x mg. x = 0.7 × 53.5 = 37.45 mg/mL.

steps.text: Calculate the required volume in mL

questions.question: A person is to receive 42 mg of calcium chloride per kg of body weight. If the person weighs 154 pounds, how many mL of a sterile solution of calcium chloride containing 1.5 mEq/mL should be administered? (CaCl<sub>2</sub> molecular weight is 111). Round final answer to the nearest tenth.

steps.text: Convert pounds to kilograms and calculate total mg needed
steps.hint: Convert the patient's weight from pounds to kilograms, then multiply by the dose per kg to get the total mg needed. 154 lb / 2.2 = 70 kg. 42 mg/kg × 70 kg = 2940 mg.

steps.text: Convert 1.5 mEq/mL to mg/mL
steps.hint: 1 mEq = 111 mg / 2 = 55.5 mg (valence of CaCl2 = 2). 1 mEq / 55.5 mg = 1.5 mEq / x mg. x = 1.5 × 55.5 = 83.25 mg/mL.

steps.text: Calculate the required volume in mL

questions.question: Patient AB receives 26 mg of ammonium chloride per kilogram of her body weight. If patient AB weighs 132 lb, how many milliliters of a sterile solution of ammonium chloride containing 0.5 mEq/mL should be administered? (NH<sub>4</sub>Cl molecular weight is 53.5). Round the final answer to the nearest tenth.

steps.text: Convert pounds to kilograms and calculate total mg needed
steps.hint: Convert the patient's weight from pounds to kilograms, then multiply by the dose per kg to get the total mg needed. 132 lb / 2.2 = 60 kg. 26 mg/kg × 60 kg = 1560 mg.

steps.text: Convert 0.5 mEq/mL to mg/mL
steps.hint: 1 mEq = 53.5 mg (valence of NH4Cl = 1). 1 mEq / 53.5 mg = 0.5 mEq / x mg. x = 0.5 × 53.5 = 26.75 mg/mL.

steps.text: Calculate the required volume in mL

questions.question: How many milliliters of a 20% (w/v) KCl solution should be used to make 1 L of solution containing 40 mEq/L KCl? (KCl m.w.= 74.5). Round the final answer to the nearest tenth.

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of KCl is 1 because it has 1 positive charge and 1 negative charge. From the question, we know that the MW of KCl is 74.5 mg, so 1 mEq = 74.5 mg / 1 = 74.5 mg.

steps.text: Use ratio and proportion to find the total mg (or g) needed for 1 L
Use the concentration of the stock solution to find the required volume

questions.question: How many milliliters of a solution containing 3 mEq of potassium chloride per milliliter should be used to obtain 3.98 g of potassium chloride? (Potassium chloride molecular weight is 74.5). Round the final answer to the nearest tenth.

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of KCl is 1 because it has 1 positive charge and 1 negative charge. From the question, we know that the MW of KCl is 74.5 mg, so 1 mEq = 74.5 mg / 1 = 74.5 mg.

steps.text: Convert grams to mg and calculate mEq needed
steps.hint: Convert 3.98 g to mg: 3.98 g × 1000 = 3980 mg. Then convert to mEq: 3980 mg ÷ 74.5 mg/mEq = 53.4 mEq.

steps.text: Calculate the required volume in mL
steps.hint: 3 mEq / 1 mL = 53.4 mEq / x mL. x = 53.4 mEq / 3 mEq/mL = 17.8 mL.

questions.question: A certain electrolyte solution contains, as one of the ingredients, the equivalent of 6.4 mEq of calcium per liter. How many grams of calcium chloride should be used in preparing 25 liters of the solution? (CaCl<sub>2</sub>∙H<sub>2</sub>O molecular weight is 147). Round the final answer to the nearest hundredth.

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of CaCl<sub>2</sub>∙H<sub>2</sub>O is 2 because calcium has a +2 charge. From the question, we know that the MW of CaCl<sub>2</sub>∙H<sub>2</sub>O is 147 mg, so 1 mEq = 147 mg / 2 = 73.5 mg.

steps.text: Use dimensional analysis to calculate total grams needed
steps.hint: Use dimensional analysis: 1 g/1000 mg × 73.5 mg/1 mEq × 6.4 mEq/1L × 25 L = 11.76 g.

questions.question: How many grams of sodium chloride should be used in preparing 3 liters of a solution containing 150 mEq/L? (NaCl molecular weight is 58.5). Round the final answer to the nearest tenth.

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of NaCl is 1 because it has 1 positive charge and 1 negative charge. From the question, we know that the MW of NaCl is 58.5 mg, so 1 mEq = 58.5 mg / 1 = 58.5 mg.

steps.text: Use ratio and proportion to calculate total grams needed
steps.hint: Use dimensional analysis: 1 g/1000 mg × 58.5 mg/1 mEq × 150 mEq/1L × 3 L = 26.3 g.

questions.question: How many grams of sodium bicarbonate should be used in preparing a liter of a solution to contain 89.2 mEq per 100 mL? (NaHCO<sub>3</sub> molecular weight is 84). Round the final answer to the nearest tenth.

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of NaHCO<sub>3</sub> is 1 because it has 1 positive charge and 1 negative charge. From the question, we know that the MW of NaHCO<sub>3</sub> is 84 mg, so 1 mEq = 84 mg / 1 = 84 mg.

steps.text: Use ratio and proportion to calculate total grams needed
steps.hint: Use dimensional analysis: 1 g/1000 mg × 84 mg/1 mEq × 89.2 mEq/100 mL × 1000 mL/1L = 74.9 g.

questions.question: How many grams of calcium chloride should be used in preparing a liter of a solution to contain 36 mEq per 50 mL? (CaCl<sub>2</sub> ∙ 2H<sub>2</sub>O molecular weight is 147). Round the final answer to the nearest tenth.

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of CaCl<sub>2</sub> ∙ 2H<sub>2</sub>O is 2 because calcium has a +2 charge. From the question, we know that the MW of CaCl<sub>2</sub> ∙ 2H<sub>2</sub>O is 147 mg, so 1 mEq = 147 mg / 2 = 73.5 mg.

steps.text: Use ratio and proportion to calculate total grams needed
steps.hint: Use dimensional analysis: 1 g/1000 mg × 73.5 mg/1 mEq × 36 mEq/50 mL × 1000 mL/1L = 52.9 g.

questions.question: Sterile solutions of ammonium chloride containing 22.47 mg/mL are available commercially in 500- and 1000-mL intravenous infusion containers. Calculate the amount, in terms of milliequivalents, of ammonium chloride in the 500-mL container. (NH<sub>4</sub>Cl molecular weight is 53.5).

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of NH<sub>4</sub>Cl is 1 because it has 1 positive charge and 1 negative charge. From the question, we know that the MW of NH<sub>4</sub>Cl is 53.5 mg, so 1 mEq = 53.5 mg / 1 = 53.5 mg.

steps.text: Use ratio and proportion to calculate total mEq in container
steps.hint: Use dimensional analysis: 1 mEq/53.5 mg × 22.47 mg/1 mL × 500 mL = 210 mEq.

questions.question: Rx Potassium Chloride 10% Cherry Syrup qs ad 520 mL Sig. 1 Tablespoonful b.i.d. How many milliequivalents of potassium chloride are represented in each prescribed dose? (KCl molecular weight is 74.5). Round the final answer to the nearest tenth.

steps.text: Calculate the dose for one prescribed dose

operands3.value: Solve for x using ratio and proportion

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of KCl is 1 because it has 1 positive charge and 1 negative charge. From the question, we know that the MW of KCl is 74.5 mg, so 1 mEq = 74.5 mg.

steps.text: Convert the dose of 1.5 g to mEq
steps.hint: 1 mEq/74.5 mg = x mEq/1500 mg. x = 1500 mg / 74.5 mg/mEq = 20.1 mEq.

questions.question:
Rx Potassium Chloride 15%
Cherry Syrup qs ad 520 mL
Sig. Teaspoonful b.i.d.
How many milliequivalents of potassium chloride are represented in each prescribed dose? (KCl molecular weight is 74.5). Round the final answer to the nearest hundredth.

steps.text: Calculate the dose for one prescribed dose
Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of KCl is 1 because it has 1 positive charge and 1 negative charge. From the question, we know that the MW of KCl is 74.5 mg, so 1 mEq = 74.5 mg.

steps.text: Convert the dose of 0.75 g to mEq
steps.hint: 1 mEq/74.5 mg = X mEq/750 mg. X = 750 mg / 74.5 mg/mEq = 10.07 mEq.

questions.question: A sterile solution of KCl contains 2 mEq per mL. If a 50 mL ampule of the solution is diluted to a liter with sterile distilled water, what is the percentage strength of the resulting solution (KCl molecular weight is 74.5)?

steps.text: Change 2 mEq/mL to mg/mL
steps.hint: 1 mEq/74.5 mg = 2 mEq/x mg. x = 2 × 74.5 = 149 mg/mL.

steps.text: Use Q1 × C1 = Q2 × C2
Convert from mg/mL to % (w/v)

questions.question: A patient is given 135 mg of phenytoin sodium three times a day. How many milliequivalents of sodium are represented in the daily dose of phenytoin sodium? (C<sub>15</sub>H<sub>11</sub>N<sub>2</sub>NaO<sub>2</sub> molecular weight is 274).

steps.text: Calculate the total daily dose
steps.hint: Multiply the single dose by the number of doses per day: 135 mg × 3 = 405 mg per day.

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of sodium is 1. From the question, the MW of phenytoin sodium is 274 mg, so 1 mEq = 274 mg / 1 = 274 mg.

steps.text: Convert the daily dose to mEq
steps.hint: 1 mEq/274 mg = X mEq/405 mg. X = 405 mg / 274 mg/mEq = 1.48 mEq.

questions.question: Patient TP is given 105 mg of phenytoin sodium four times a day. How many milliequivalents of sodium are represented in the daily dose of phenytoin sodium? (C<sub>15</sub>H<sub>11</sub>N<sub>2</sub>NaO<sub>2</sub> molecular weight is 274).

steps.text: Calculate the total daily dose
steps.hint: Multiply the single dose by the number of doses per day: 105 mg × 4 = 420 mg per day.

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of sodium is 1. From the question, the MW of phenytoin sodium is 274 mg, so 1 mEq = 274 mg / 1 = 274 mg.

steps.text: Convert the daily dose to mEq
steps.hint: 1 mEq/274 mg = X mEq/420 mg. X = 420 mg / 274 mg/mEq = 1.53 mEq.

questions.question: A solution contains 30 mg% (30 mg/100 mL) of citrate ions (C<sub>6</sub>H<sub>5</sub>O<sub>7</sub><sup>3-</sup>). Express this concentration in terms of milliequivalents per liter. (Citrate ion molecular weight is 189).

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of citrate ion is 3 (indicated by 3- in the formula). From the question, we know that the MW of citrate ion is 189 mg, so 1 mEq = 189 mg / 3 = 63 mg.

steps.text: Convert concentration from mg% to mg/L
Convert mg/L to mEq/L using ratio and proportion
steps.hint: 1 mEq/63 mg = X mEq/300 mg. X = 300 mg × (1 mEq/63 mg) = 4.76 mEq/L.

questions.question: A solution contains 40 mg% (40 mg/100 mL) of calcium ions (Ca<sup>2+</sup>). Express this concentration in terms of milliequivalents per liter. (Ca atomic weight is 40).

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of calcium ion is 2 (indicated by Ca<sup>2+</sup>). From the question, we know that the atomic weight of Ca is 40 mg, so 1 mEq = 40 mg / 2 = 20 mg.

steps.text: Convert concentration from mg% to mg/L
Convert mg/L to mEq/L using ratio and proportion
steps.hint: 1 mEq/20 mg = X mEq/400 mg. X = 400 mg × (1 mEq/20 mg) = 20 mEq/L.

questions.question: A sterile solution of CaCO<sub>3</sub> contains 5 mEq per mL. If a 150 mL ampule of the solution is diluted to a liter with sterile distilled water, what is the percentage strength of the resulting solution (CaCO<sub>3</sub> molecular weight is 100)?

steps.text: Change 5 mEq/mL to mg/mL
steps.hint: For CaCO<sub>3</sub>, MW = 100 and valence = 2, so 1 mEq = 100/2 = 50 mg. Therefore, 1 mEq/50 mg = 5 mEq/x mg. x = 5 × 50 = 250 mg/mL.

steps.text: Use Q1 × C1 = Q2 × C2
Convert from mg/mL to % (w/v)

questions.question: A magnesium (Mg<sup>2+</sup>) level in blood plasma is determined to be 2.75 mEq/L. Express this concentration in terms of milligrams per liter. (Magnesium atomic weight is 24).

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of magnesium ion is 2 (indicated by Mg<sup>2+</sup>). From the question, we know that the atomic weight of Mg is 24 mg, so 1 mEq = 24 mg / 2 = 12 mg.

steps.text: Use ratio and proportion to convert from mEq/L to mg/L
steps.hint: 1 mEq/12 mg = 2.75 mEq/x mg. x = 2.75 mEq × 12 mg/mEq = 33 mg/L.

questions.question: What is the concentration, in g per mL, of a solution containing 6 mEq of magnesium sulfate (MgSO<sub>4</sub> · 2H<sub>2</sub>O) per mL? (MgSO<sub>4</sub> · 2H<sub>2</sub>O molecular weight is 156.4).

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of MgSO<sub>4</sub> · 2H<sub>2</sub>O is 2 because of the Mg<sup>2+</sup> ion. From the question, we know that the MW is 156.4 mg, so 1 mEq = 156.4 mg / 2 = 78.2 mg.

steps.text: Convert 6 mEq/mL to mg/mL
Convert from mg/mL to g/mL

questions.question: A nutritional solution contains 25 mg/100 mL of Ca<sup>2+</sup> ions. Express this concentration in terms of milliequivalents per liter. (Calcium atomic weight is 40).

steps.text: Identify the valence and calculate how many mg = 1 mEq
steps.hint: The valence of calcium ion is 2 (indicated by Ca<sup>2+</sup>). From the question, we know that the atomic weight of Ca is 40 mg, so 1 mEq = 40 mg / 2 = 20 mg.

steps.text: Convert concentration from mg/100 mL to mg/L
Convert mg/L to mEq/L using ratio and proportion
steps.hint: 1 mEq/20 mg = X mEq/250 mg. X = 250 mg × (1 mEq/20 mg) = 12.5 mEq/L.

questions.question: An intravenous infusion is to contain 40 mEq of potassium ion and 55 mEq of sodium ion in 500 mL of 5% dextrose injection. Using potassium chloride injection (6 g/30 mL) and 23.4% sodium chloride injection, how many milliliters of each should be used to supply the required ions in the IV infusion? (NaCl molecular weight is 58.5; KCl molecular weight is 74.5).

steps.text: Calculate the concentration of KCl injection in mEq/mL
steps.hint: 6 g/30 mL = 0.2 g/mL. For KCl, 1 mEq = 74.5 mg. 0.2 g/mL × (1000 mg/1 g) × (1 mEq/74.5 mg) = 2.68 mEq/mL

steps.text: Calculate the volume of KCl injection needed
steps.hint: 2.68 mEq/1 mL = 40 mEq/x mL. x = 40 mEq / 2.68 mEq/mL = 14.9 mL

steps.text: Calculate the concentration of NaCl injection in mEq/mL
Calculate the volume of NaCl injection needed
steps.hint: 4.0 mEq/1 mL = 55 mEq/x mL. x = 55 mEq / 4.0 mEq/mL = 13.75 mL
